glossary.hub: A Hub stores the unique business identifiers for a core business concept (e.g. a product, a vendor, a warehouse). Each hub has one row per distinct business key — no duplicates. Hubs do NOT store descriptive attributes, only the key. They are the anchor points of the vault that everything else connects to.
glossary.link: A Link records a relationship between two or more hubs. For example, a link between a product hub and a vendor hub says 'this product was ordered from this vendor.' Links store only hash keys pointing to the hubs they connect — no descriptive attributes. One row per unique combination of the connected hub keys.
glossary.satellite: A Satellite stores the descriptive attributes (the 'facts') that belong to a hub or link. For example, a product satellite stores the product name, price, and weight. Satellites are historized: when a value changes, a new row is inserted rather than the old row being updated. Each row has a load date and an end date so you can see what the data looked like at any point in time.
glossary.mart: An Information Mart (mart) is a business-friendly, pre-joined view of vault data optimized for answering a specific business question. Marts use readable business key columns instead of hash keys, avoid vault-internal columns like load_end_dts, and are rebuilt on each pipeline run. A mart is the output layer that analysts query.

hub.name: <entity>_hk
hub.description: MD5 hash of the business key. Primary key of the hub. Named after the entity (e.g. product_hk for hub_product).

hub.name: <entity>_bk
hub.description: The raw business key value from the source system (e.g. the product_id string from erp.products). Must never be NULL.

hub.name: load_dts
hub.description: Timestamp when this record was first loaded into the vault.

hub.name: record_source
hub.description: Identifies which source system this record came from (see record_sources list).

link.name: <link_name>_hk
link.description: MD5 hash of the concatenated hub hash keys that this link connects. Primary key of the link.

link.name: <hub1>_hk
link.description: FK — hash key of the first hub being linked.

link.name: <hub2>_hk
link.description: FK — hash key of the second hub being linked. Add more hub FK columns if the link connects more than two hubs.

link.name: load_dts
link.description: Timestamp when this relationship was first loaded into the vault.

link.name: record_source
link.description: Identifies which source system this record came from.

satellite.name: <parent>_hk
satellite.description: FK — hash key of the parent hub or link this satellite describes.

satellite.name: load_dts
satellite.description: Timestamp when this version of the record was loaded. Together with the parent hash key this forms the composite primary key.

satellite.name: load_end_dts
satellite.description: Timestamp when this record was superseded by a newer version. NULL means this is the current active record.

satellite.name: record_source
satellite.description: Identifies which source system this record came from.

satellite.name: ... (descriptive columns)
satellite.description: All descriptive/attribute columns from the source system that belong to this satellite's subject area.

mart.name: ... (business key columns)
mart.description: Use _bk suffixed business key columns from the relevant hubs — never hash keys.

mart.name: ... (metric/attribute columns)
mart.description: All measure and descriptive columns needed to answer the target business question.

mart.name: mart_insert_dts
mart.description: Audit column recording when this mart row was inserted. Always the last column.

hash.composite_key_expression: md5(UPPER(TRIM(CAST(col1 AS VARCHAR))) || '|' || UPPER(TRIM(CAST(col2 AS VARCHAR))))
hash.notes: Always UPPER and TRIM the input before hashing to ensure consistency. Use '|' as the separator when hashing composite keys.

conventions.idempotent_ddl: Use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for all vault and mart tables so repeated runs do not error
conventions.satellite_historization: On load, set load_end_dts = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on existing rows before inserting new rows. Do not update or delete old rows.
conventions.hub_dedup: Insert only rows whose business key does not already exist in the hub (use WHERE NOT EXISTS or INSERT OR IGNORE pattern)
conventions.link_dedup: Insert only rows whose combined hub hash keys do not already exist in the link
conventions.mart_rebuild: Marts are rebuilt when stale or missing: DELETE all rows, then INSERT fresh. Use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS to create on first run.
conventions.mart_expiry_note: Marts older than mart_ttl_hours are rebuilt on next request. Marts older than mart_cleanup_hours are dropped automatically at the start of the next Dispatcher run.
conventions.null_handling: Never store NULL in business key columns. If a source key is NULL, exclude the row from the load.
conventions.schema: Two separate DuckDB databases are used. Source tables live in /data/source.duckdb under schema-qualified names (erp.products, procurement.vendors, wms.stock_movements, etc.) — this database is read-only and never modified by the pipeline. Vault tables (hub_*, lnk_*, sat_*), mart tables (mart_*), and temporary staging tables (stg_*) all live in the main schema (no prefix) of /data/vault.duckdb.
conventions.staging_rule: Data Vault Agent load SQL must SELECT from stg_* staging tables only — never from the original source schemas (erp.*, procurement.*, wms.*). Staging tables are created in vault.duckdb by the Dispatcher (using ATTACH to read from source.duckdb) before the Data Vault Agent runs.
conventions.staging_cleanup: Staging tables are dropped from vault.duckdb by the Dispatcher via HTTP Request to duckdb-service after the full pipeline completes (after vault and mart SQL has been executed).
